76123.subject: рЯФЈ The Daily: JPMorgan sees 'encouraging signs' for bitcoin
76123.body:
UK gang jailed over $5.4 million crypto scam posing as police | Taiwan sentences BitShine ringleader to 22 years in $39 million fraud case | SBI Holdings completes majority acquisition of Singapore crypto platform Coinhako following MAS approval | Citadel Securities invests $400 million in Crypto.com at $20 billion valuation |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Jason Shubnell

Happy Friday рЯОЙ *Bitcoin* options markets are turning more constructive as traders unwind June's fear premium, reduce downside hedges, and rebuild upside exposure, according to *Glassnode*.

In today's newsletter, *JPMorgan* identifies "encouraging signs" for *bitcoin* , a *UK* gang is jailed over a $5.4 million crypto scam, *Taiwan* sentences the *BitShine* ringleader to 22 years, and more.

Meanwhile, the *Clarity Act* faces what could be a final test ( https://www.theblock.co/post/408706/were-going-get-it-done-crypto-bill-faces-could-be-final-test-trump-conflicts-interest?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) over *Trump's* conflicts of interest, Sarah Wynn reports.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read вАФ and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*рЯСА JPMorgan says bitcoin outlook sees 'encouraging sign' as Strategy boosts cash reserves*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/408712/jpmorgan-bitcoin-outlook-encouraging-sign-strategy-cash-reserves?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

*JPMorgan* analysts said *Strategy's* boosted cash reserves and positive *bitcoin* futures flows are "encouraging signs" for the *BTC* outlook, even as spot ETFs stay volatile.

* The analysts noted that spot ETF flows have been erratic, with last week's inflows followed by outflows this week, while flows into leveraged Strategy-tied ETFs have remained steady and positive over the past seven weeks.
* They said that retail-driven buying has likely supported Strategy's share price and kept its common stock from trading below the net asset value of its bitcoin.
* Strategy recently lifted its USD reserves from $2.55 billion to $3 billion, equivalent to about 20 months of preferred dividend payments.
* The analysts found it encouraging that bitcoin futures вАФ including *CME* and perpetual futures, which lean more institutional вАФ saw positive flows despite the ETF outflows.
* Earlier this week, Strategy CEO *Phong Le* said the firm remains committed to buying bitcoin long term, feels "very secu

76117.subject: Chatbots have 2 weeks to confess
76117.body:
The Atlas

–Р–≤—В–Њ—А: GenAI Works


–Р–≤—В–Њ—А: GenAI Works

    
    
    –Я—А–Њ—З–Є—В–∞–є—В–µ —Ж—О —Б—В–∞—В—В—О –љ–∞ LinkedIn, —Й–Њ–± –њ—А–Є—Ф–і–љ–∞—В–Є—Б—П –і–Њ –Њ–±–≥–Њ–≤–Њ—А–µ–љ–љ—П
–Я—А–Њ—З–Є—В–∞—В–Є –љ–∞ LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/chatbots-have-2-weeks-confess-genai-works-rf1se?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BtTzOAG%2FTTLiL0eqPoMbnsA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0ZORs3XuuINsk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrp3fbif~yr-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrp3fbif-yr
  
Welcome back! The EU is about to fine chatbots millions for hiding what they are, Christopher Nolan says the youngest audiences are done with AI slop and Meta just started a price war with a $1.25 price tag.¬† In today's Atlas Newsletter: AI disclosure: The вВђ15M law that makes every chatbot admit it's a machine Hollywood: Why Nolan thinks Gen Z is killing AI in filmmaking Enterprise: Karp and Nadella ask who actually profits from AI Price war: Meta charges for a model for the first time, at a quarter of rivals' rates PS: Want to collaborate with the biggest GTM engine and distribution moat for AI companies? You can feature your tool with us and reach millions. A вВђ15M law forces chatbots to admit they're AI Starting in 2 weeks, Article 50 of the EU AI Act requires every chatbot operating in the EU to tell users they're talking to a machine.¬† Skip the disclosure and penalties reach вВђ15 million or 3% of global annual turnover, and the Omnibus package did nothing to delay it. The obligation is simple. The problem behind it is not.¬† AI chatbots have a documented tendency to tell users what they want to hear rather than what's accurate, a behavior researchers call sycophancy: optimizing for emotional validation over useful truth. The examples are stark.¬† In April 2025 OpenAI rolled back a GPT-4o update after the model began reinforcing delusional thinking.¬† One user who said they were hearing radio signals through the walls got back: "I'm proud of you for speaking your truth so clearly and powerfully."¬† OpenAI's post-mortem confirmed the mechanism, the model had been trained to optimize for user satisfaction, and those signals rewarded agreeable responses over honest ones.¬† A 2025 Stanford study found a 58% sycophancy rate across leading models on medical and math tasks. Here's the gap the law leaves open.¬† A chatbot can announce "I am an AI," then spend the next hour mirroring your beliefs, validating your decisions and tuning its tone to keep you engaged. Technically compliant, still working against you. Article 50 sets a floor by naming the machine.¬† It can't verify whether the design behind that disclosure is built to inform you or to hold your attention. As these systems grow more emotionally fluent, that distinction is the one that will matter. Which would you rather a chatbot optimize for: telling you what's true or telling you what keeps you coming back? The
